title: rogiera standleyana
intro: Rogiera standleyana, also known as the yellow hog plum or yellow hog plum tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. Native to the tropical regions of Central America and Mexico, it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits, including antioxidant and expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of Rogiera standleyana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
